title: Unlock the Power of Cloud Services with EstonSoft
paragraph: Looking to elevate your business with secure, scalable, and reliable cloud solutions? EstonSoft has you covered with custom integration and migration services.
content:
In today's hyper-connected business environment, cloud services have shifted from being a competitive advantage to an operational necessity. As organizations look to increase efficiency, reduce costs, and scale dynamically, cloud infrastructure provides the foundational framework to make these goals achievable.

Custom Cloud Solutions Tailored to Your Needs

Every business operates under unique conditions. At EstonSoft, we reject the one-size-fits-all approach. We design custom cloud solutions that align with your specific goals, ensuring seamless integration with your existing legacy systems while optimizing performance for future demands.

Seamless Cloud Migration

Transitioning to the cloud can seem daunting due to potential downtime and security concerns. Our team of certified engineers handles the end-to-end migration process, safeguarding data integrity and ensuring zero operational disruption. From lift-and-shift migrations to refactoring workloads for cloud-native architectures, we manage every phase with precision.

Scalable Infrastructure

Growth should not be limited by hardware constraints. With modern cloud environments, your business can scale resource allocation dynamically to match demand. This pay-as-you-go flexibility eliminates the need for expensive upfront capital expenditure on physical servers.

Advanced Security and Compliance

Data security is at the core of everything we build. We implement multi-layered encryption, robust identity management, and strict access controls to protect your intellectual property. Additionally, we ensure that your architecture complies with industry standards such as GDPR, HIPAA, or PCI-DSS.

Managed Services and Continuous Support

Cloud infrastructure requires ongoing maintenance, monitoring, and optimization. Our managed cloud services take this burden off your shoulders. We provide 24/7 monitoring, patch management, threat detection, and performance tuning so your team can stay focused on core business innovations.

Whether your strategy involves Amazon Web Services (AWS), Microsoft Azure, Google Cloud Platform (GCP), or hybrid cloud setups, EstonSoft has the expertise to guide your journey. Let us turn your vision into a highly reliable digital reality.

title: Elevate Your Software Quality with EstonSoft
paragraph: Deliver flawless digital experiences with rigorous software testing. Explore how manual and automated QA strategies can protect your brand and delight users.
content:
In the digital economy, software quality is directly tied to business success. A single critical bug can lead to lost revenue, reputational damage, and frustrated users. Implementing a rigorous quality assurance (QA) pipeline is essential to delivering robust software applications that perform reliably under any conditions.

Comprehensive Manual Testing

While automation handles repetitive tasks, human intuition remains irreplaceable. Our manual testing services focus on exploratory, usability, and ad-hoc testing. Experienced QA professionals navigate your application from a user's perspective, discovering edge cases and visual defects that automated scripts might overlook.

Automated Testing for Speed and Scale

To support rapid deployment cycles and continuous integration, we design automated testing frameworks. By writing robust scripts for regression, unit, and API testing, we drastically reduce test execution times. This allows your development team to receive immediate feedback and release updates with confidence.

Performance and Load Testing

An application that runs fine with ten users might collapse under ten thousand. We run extensive load, stress, and performance testing to identify bottlenecks, memory leaks, and latency issues before your software goes live. This ensures your systems remain responsive during high-traffic events.

Security Testing and Vulnerability Assessment

Securing sensitive user data is paramount. We conduct detailed vulnerability assessments and security checks to identify potential entry points for unauthorized access. By adhering to industry-standard security practices, we help you patch vulnerabilities before they can be exploited.

Success Highlight: We recently assisted a growing e-commerce platform prepare for a major seasonal flash sale. Through rigorous performance tuning and load testing, we helped scale their infrastructure capacity by 5x, enabling a flawless user experience and zero downtime during their busiest sales day.

Quality is never an accident; it is the result of intelligent effort and structured testing methodologies. Partner with EstonSoft to ensure your software meets the highest industry standards.

title: Unlocking Business Growth with Web App Solutions
paragraph: Custom web applications are key to streamlining operations, expanding customer reach, and driving growth. Discover the strategic advantages of web-based software.
content:
The modern business landscape requires agility and connectivity. As user expectations shift toward instant, cross-device access, businesses must adopt technology that operates seamlessly anywhere. Custom web application solutions provide the tools necessary to automate manual workflows, engage clients globally, and establish a scalable foundation for business growth.

Strategic Benefits of Custom Web Applications

Unlike off-the-shelf software, custom web applications are built specifically around your business processes. Here is how they drive efficiency:

  • Cross-Platform Reach: Accessible via any modern browser, web apps eliminate the need to build separate native apps for iOS, Android, and desktop.
  • Operational Efficiency: Automating repetitive workflows reduces manual errors, saving your team hours of administrative work.
  • Data Centralization: Keeping data in a centralized database ensures your team works with real-time information, improving decision-making speed.
  • Scalability: Custom web apps can easily scale to accommodate more users, new features, and integrations as your business expands.

Real-World Impacts of Custom Web Applications

Modern web solutions solve concrete business challenges across various sectors:

  • E-commerce Growth: Custom portals improve user retention and checkout conversion rates by offering tailored user journeys and localized payment integrations.
  • Logistics and Supply Chain: Interactive inventory and dispatch tracking systems reduce delivery delays and optimize routing.
  • Customer Retention: Portals that feature self-service capabilities increase user satisfaction and lower customer support ticket volumes.

Key Considerations for Building a Web App

When planning a custom web application, focus on creating an intuitive user interface, applying robust security protocols like HTTPS and token authentication, and building a modular architecture that supports future feature additions.

At EstonSoft, we combine industry experience with cutting-edge front-end and back-end web frameworks to deliver software that solves complex business problems. Contact us to learn how we can design a solution tailored to your operational goals.

title: How to Deploy a React App to a Subdirectory on Hostinger Shared Hosting
paragraph: Learn how to deploy a React application under a subdirectory (such as /admin) on a Hostinger shared hosting account, including essential .htaccess routing rules.
content:
Deploying a React application under a subdirectory (e.g., https://yourdomain.com/admin) on Hostinger shared hosting is a highly practical way to host internal tools, admin consoles, or client dashboards without buying additional domains. This setup allows you to run a main website on your root domain while maintaining separate, independent single-page applications (SPAs) in subfolders.

In this guide, we will walk you through the complete deployment configuration process, focusing on directory setup, homepage settings, and server routing overrides.

Step 1: Set the Homepage and Build the Application

By default, Vite or Create React App assumes the application will run at the root of a domain. To host the application in a subdirectory, you must configure the base URL path before compilation.

For Vite Projects:

Open your vite.config.js file and add the base property:

export default defineConfig({
  base: '/admin/',
  plugins: [react()],
});

For Create React App Projects:

Open your package.json and set the homepage property:

"homepage": "https://yourdomain.com/admin"

After saving these changes, build your project for production by running:

npm run build

Step 2: Upload Files to the Directory Structure

Next, you need to prepare the folder structure in your Hostinger account:

  • Log in to your Hostinger hPanel and navigate to the File Manager.
  • Open the public_html directory of your root domain.
  • Create a new directory named admin (or your preferred subdirectory name).
  • Upload all contents of your project's build/ or dist/ output folder directly into the newly created admin/ directory. Do not upload the build folder itself, only the files inside it.

Step 3: Configure URL Rewrites with .htaccess

If your application uses React Router, accessing subpaths (like https://yourdomain.com/admin/settings) directly will return a 404 error from the Apache server. This happens because the server searches for a physical directory named "settings" instead of routing the request to React's entry point.

To resolve this, create a file named .htaccess inside the public_html/admin/ directory and paste the following rewrite configuration:

<IfModule mod_rewrite.c>
  RewriteEngine On
  RewriteBase /admin/
  RewriteRule ^index\.html$ - [L]
  RewriteCond %{REQUEST_FILENAME} !-f
  RewriteCond %{REQUEST_FILENAME} !-d
  RewriteRule . /admin/index.html [L]
</IfModule>

Summary

By defining the base path in your builder configuration and adding a dedicated .htaccess file to redirect traffic to your subfolder's index.html, you enable client-side routing. Your React application is now successfully hosted under a subdirectory.

title: How to Deploy a Next.js Site to the Root Domain on Hostinger Shared Hosting
paragraph: Discover how to export your Next.js application as a static site and deploy it directly to the root domain of a Hostinger shared hosting environment.
content:
Next.js is a powerful framework for building modern web applications, but hosting it on shared platforms like Hostinger can pose challenges since shared plans do not always support running continuous Node.js processes. Fortunately, for static or client-rendered projects, you can use Next.js's Static HTML Export feature. This compiles your pages into static HTML, CSS, and JS assets, which can run on any standard web server without a Node.js runtime.

Step 1: Configure Next.js for Static Export

To instruct Next.js to export your project as static assets, open your next.config.js (or next.config.mjs) file and update the configuration to include the output parameter:

/** @type {import('next').NextConfig} */
const nextConfig = {
  output: 'export',
  // Optional: Disable server-side image optimization since we are running static
  images: {
    unoptimized: true,
  },
};

module.exports = nextConfig;

Step 2: Generate the Build Assets

Run the build script in your project directory:

npm run build

This command compiles your application and creates a directory named out in your root folder. This folder contains all the static HTML files, styles, and javascript bundles required to run your site.

Step 3: Upload the Build Files to public_html

Use Hostinger hPanel's File Manager or an FTP client to transfer the static assets:

  • Navigate to the public_html directory of your root domain.
  • Clear any placeholder files (such as Hostinger's default default.php or index.html).
  • Upload all the files and folders from inside your local out directory directly to public_html.

Step 4: Enable Routing Fallback with .htaccess

If your Next.js project uses dynamic page routing, you need to configure the server to fallback gracefully for missing paths. Create a .htaccess file in the public_html directory with the following rewrite rules:

RewriteEngine On
RewriteCond %{DOCUMENT_ROOT}/$1.html -f
RewriteRule ^([^.]+)/?$ /$1.html [L]
RewriteCond %{REQUEST_FILENAME} !-f
RewriteCond %{REQUEST_FILENAME} !-d
RewriteRule . /404.html [L]

This setup matches URLs to their corresponding HTML file and falls back to your custom 404.html page if the resource does not exist, providing a smooth user experience.

title: Case Study: FastAPI Application Deployment on Ubuntu 24.04 VPS
paragraph: Follow this detailed guide on deploying a production-ready FastAPI application on an Ubuntu 24.04 VPS using Gunicorn, systemd, MongoDB, and Nginx.
content:
Deploying a Python web framework like FastAPI on a Virtual Private Server (VPS) provides full control over performance, configuration, and security. For a production deployment, running FastAPI in a developer sandbox is not sufficient. You need a system that starts automatically on boot, handles incoming web traffic efficiently, and manages active database connections securely.

This case study details the process of configuring an Ubuntu 24.04 VPS on Hostinger to run a FastAPI backend connected to MongoDB, fronted by Nginx as a reverse proxy.

Step 1: Secure the Server and Setup Dependencies

First, update the system packages and install python dependencies:

sudo apt update && sudo apt upgrade -y
sudo apt install python3-pip python3-venv git nginx -y

Step 2: Install and Configure MongoDB

To install MongoDB on Ubuntu 24.04, import the repository keys, add the MongoDB library to apt sources, and install the database engine:

sudo apt install gnupg curl -y
curl -fsSL https://www.mongodb.org/static/pgp/server-7.0.asc | sudo gpg --dearmor -o /usr/share/keyrings/mongodb-server-keyring.gpg
echo "deb [ arch=amd64,arm64 signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/mongodb-server-keyring.gpg ] https://repo.mongodb.org/apt/ubuntu noble/mongodb-org/7.0 multiverse" | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/mongodb-org-7.0.list
sudo apt update
sudo apt install -y mongodb-org
sudo systemctl enable --now mongod

Step 3: Setup the FastAPI Project and Virtual Environment

Clone your repository and set up a clean Python virtual environment to manage dependencies:

git clone https://github.com/yourusername/yourproject.git /var/www/myapi
cd /var/www/myapi
python3 -m venv venv
source venv/bin/activate
pip install -r requirements.txt gunicorn uvicorn

Step 4: Create a Systemd Service File

To run the application in the background and ensure it recovers from system reboots, configure a systemd service manager daemon. Create /etc/systemd/system/fastapi.service:

[Unit]
Description=Gunicorn instance to serve FastAPI
After=network.target

[Service]
User=www-data
Group=www-data
WorkingDirectory=/var/www/myapi
Environment="PATH=/var/www/myapi/venv/bin"
ExecStart=/var/www/myapi/venv/bin/gunicorn -w 4 -k uvicorn.workers.UvicornWorker main:app --bind 127.0.0.1:8000

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target

Enable and start the service:

sudo systemctl daemon-reload
sudo systemctl enable --now fastapi

Step 5: Configure Nginx as a Reverse Proxy

Gunicorn handles Python requests, but a web server like Nginx is recommended to manage incoming network traffic, serve static files, and handle SSL handshake verification. Create a configuration file in /etc/nginx/sites-available/myapi:

server {
    listen 80;
    server_name api.yourdomain.com;

    location / {
        proxy_pass http://127.0.0.1:8000;
        proxy_set_header Host $host;
        proxy_set_header X-Real-IP $remote_addr;
        proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for;
        proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Proto $scheme;
    }
}

Enable the site configuration and restart Nginx:

sudo ln -s /etc/nginx/sites-available/myapi /etc/nginx/sites-enabled/
sudo systemctl restart nginx

Conclusion

You now have a secure, production-grade architecture serving your FastAPI application. Nginx listens on port 80, forwards traffic to the internal Gunicorn process, which communicates with the local MongoDB database, all managed reliably under systemd service configuration.
